folio.label: 153r
folio.l: Come doun to day , and late ©oure bagges stonde
Ne be ©e nat a shamed , that Daun Ioún
Shal fastynge al this day , elenge goon
What late vs heere a masse , and go we dyne
Wyf q¶ this man , litel canstow deuyne
The curious besynesse , that we haue
For of vs chapmen , also god me saue
And by that lord , that cleped is Seynt Yue
Scarsly a monges twewe , ten shuln thryue
Contynuely lastyngö , vn to oure age
We moun wel make chiere , and good visage
And dryue fortú the world , as it may be
And kepen oure estat , in pryuetee
Til we be ded , or elles that we pleye
A Pilgrimage , or gon out of the weye
And therfore haue I , gret necessite
Vp on this queynt world , to auyse me
For euäe mot we , stonde in drede
Of hap and fortune , in oure chapmanhede
To Flaundres wol I go , to morwe atat day
And come a geyn as sone , as euäe I may
For whicú my deere wyf , I the by seke

note.notetxt: I the241 A space marker separates these two words. Double check whether the word has been inserted.Est/ODR

folio.l: bAsbAsbe to euäy wygút , buxome & meke242 The line-initial capital looks like it has been corrected from another letter. Double check for app tagJRT/ODR
FAndFAnd for to kepe oure good , be curious
And honestly gouäne wel , oure hous
Thow hast I now , in euäy manere wyse
That to a thrifty houshold , may suffise
The lakketú noon array , ne no vitaille
Of Siluer in thy purs , shaltow nat faille
And witú that word , his Countour dore he shette
And doun he gotú , no lengere wold he lette
But hastily , a masse , was there seyde
And spedily the tables , were I leyde
And to the dynere , faste they he~ spedde
And richely this Monk , the Chapman fedde
¢ At after dyner , Daun Ioún sobrely
This Chapman toke a part , and preuyly
He seid hi~ thus , Cosyn it stonditú so
That wel I se , to Brugges wyl ©e go
God and Seynt Austyn , spede ©ow and gyde
I preye ©ow Cosyn , wysely that ©e ride
Gouänetú ©ow also , of ©oure dyete
A temprely , and namely in this hete
